about.title: About Communities
about.description: Communities are federated community organizations that set their own demurrage rates within protocol bounds (5-15% annually). Each community operates independently while sharing the same time-standard economic model.
about.membershipValue: Open to anyone, join via community interface

oracle.responsibility3: Help maintain trust in the system

home.title: KCHNG
home.description: Turn community service into shared value
home.audience: For communities, cooperatives, and collectives who want to track and reward the work that keeps them running.
home.step1Desc: Do community service — cooking, cleaning, maintenance, teaching, organizing.
home.step2Desc: Your community validates the work. No algorithms, just trust.
home.step3Desc: Receive KCHNG tokens. 30 minutes work = 1,000 KCHNG.
home.valueNote: Protocol-defined: 30 min work mints 1,000 KCHNG. Social convention: 1,000 KCHNG ≈ 1 community meal.
home.realValueDesc: 1,000 KCHNG ≈ 1 community meal (social peg). The protocol mints 1,000 KCHNG per 30 min verified work.
home.communityVerifiedDesc: Work is validated by people you know. Trust built through relationships.
home.staysActiveDesc: Currency circulates instead of sitting idle. Good for everyone.
home.madeForCommunitiesDesc: Built for eco-villages, cooperatives, housing collectives, and community spaces.
home.whyDemurrageDesc1: KCHNG is designed to keep value moving through your community. When tokens sit unused for too long, they gradually decrease — encouraging people to spend, share, or trade rather than hoard.
home.whyDemurrageDesc2: This isn't a penalty. It's how the system stays healthy. Active currency means more trades, more connections, and a stronger community economy.
home.whyDemurrageExample: Example: If you don't use your balance for a month, it might decrease by about 1%. Stay active and it stays full.
home.whoIsThisForDesc: KCHNG works best for communities that:
home.whoIsThisForItem1: Have a concept of community service or shared work
home.whoIsThisForItem2: Want to track and reward contributions fairly
home.whoIsThisForItem3: Are small enough that members know each other
home.learnMoreLink: Learn more about how KCHNG works
home.getStartedDesc: Connect your wallet to access the full KCHNG platform:

testWalletInstructions.instruction3: Write it down on paper and storing digitally (email, cloud) is risky
testWalletInstructions.instruction4: Store it somewhere safe, consider a password manager
testWalletInstructions.testWalletNote: Test wallets are for experimentation only. Never use testnet wallets for real funds.

about.title: About KCHNG
about.subtitle: Community currency for collaborative communities
about.whatIsP1: KCHNG is a time-backed community currency — money that's created by productive work, not by governments or banks. When you do verified work for your community, new KCHNG is minted directly to you. No one's account is debited; the tokens are newly created, backed by the value of your labor.
about.whatIsP2: The protocol defines: 30 minutes of verified work mints 1,000 KCHNG. Communities may peg this to whatever they choose — but for easy exchange across communities, 1,000 KCHNG ≈ 1 meal is recommended.
about.whatIsP3: KCHNG is governed by Communities — groups that set their own rules, rates, and verifiers. Each community is independent, but they share the same time standard.
about.whatIsP4: Time creates value, inactivity reduces it. When you work, you add value to the system. When tokens sit unused, they gradually decay — promoting circulation instead of hoarding.
about.comparisonIntro: KCHNG works differently from traditional money, cryptocurrency, and commodity-backed currencies:
about.comparisonQ3: What happens if I hold it?
about.comparisonKchngA3:
Gradually decays
(use it or lose it)
about.mintVsTransferIntro: When you earn KCHNG through work, new tokens are created — not transferred from someone else:
about.mintVsTransferKey: Key insight: Work performed → New value created → New tokens minted to represent that value. The "backing" is the labor itself — the tokens didn't exist before because the work hadn't been done.
about.barterVsKchngIntro: KCHNG is essentially barter made scalable — it keeps the "real value" nature of barter while solving the coordination problems:
about.barterVsKchngDirectA4: Must find someone who wants what you have
about.barterVsKchngKchngA4: Trade with anyone in the community
about.barterVsKchngKey: Bottom line: KCHNG solves the "double coincidence of wants" problem. In direct barter, I need eggs and you need bread — if we don't match, no trade. With KCHNG: I give eggs → receive KCHNG → later use KCHNG for firewood from someone else. Same real-value exchange, but flexible.
about.protocolIntro: These rules are hardcoded in the smart contract — not suggestions or guidelines. They cannot be changed by any individual, governor, or community without network-wide consensus.
about.protocolMinVerifiersNote: Each claim needs at least 2 verifiers
about.protocolApprovalNote: More than half of assigned verifiers must approve
about.protocolVerifierStakeNote: 100,000 KCHNG staked to register (≈ 50 hours work)
about.protocolGovernorPowers: Manage community membership, set demurrage rate (5–15%), register/remove verifiers
about.protocolVerifierPowers: Approve or reject work claims in their community
about.protocolOraclePowers: Activate grace periods for members in hardship (emergency, illness, community-voted)
about.protocolMembershipNote: Must leave current community before joining another. Balance transfers with you.
about.protocolDemurrageNote: Each community sets its own rate within these bounds. Default: 12%.
about.protocolCrossCommunityNote: Formula accounts for different demurrage rates between communities.
about.protocolRangeNote: Starts at 500 (neutral). Tracked per role, not globally.
about.protocolRolesNote: Governor, Verifier, Oracle, Worker, Member — each with separate reputation
about.protocolProbationNote: Score < 200 triggers probation: limited abilities, higher slashing rates
about.protocolTf2tNote: Tit-for-2-Tats: 2+ consecutive negative events = −25 bonus penalty. Prevents gaming through alternating behavior.
about.protocolSlashingNote: Verifiers: 10%, Oracles: 25% slashed when unregistering (higher if on probation)
about.whoForIntro: KCHNG is designed for communities that:
about.whoForEco: Eco-villages — Track contributions to shared infrastructure, gardening, maintenance
about.whoForCoop: Cooperatives — Reward member participation beyond formal employment
about.whoForSpaces: Community spaces — Shared venues, event spaces, gathering places
about.whoForHousing: Housing collectives — Manage shared chores and responsibilities
about.whoForAny: Any group with shared work and a need to track contributions fairly
about.keyRequirement: Key requirement: The community should be small enough that members can know and trust each other. KCHNG works through peer verification, not algorithms.
about.step1Desc: Cooking communal meals, fixing things, organizing events, teaching workshops, cleaning, gardening — anything the community values.
about.step2Desc: Other members confirm the work happened. This isn't bureaucratic — it's just people vouching for each other. Usually 2 verifiers is enough.
about.step3Desc: New KCHNG is minted directly to the worker's wallet. This isn't transferred from somewhere else — it's newly created value, backed by real work.
about.whyTimeP1: Unlike regular money, KCHNG's value is tied to actual human effort, not what the market decides. One hour of work is worth the same whether you're cooking, cleaning, or teaching.
about.whyTimeP2: This creates fairness. The person scrubbing floors isn't valued less than the person running a workshop. All work that keeps the community going is equally valuable.
about.workTypesIntro: While all work is valued, some work carries more responsibility or urgency:
about.whyDecreaseTitle: Why do tokens decrease when unused?
about.whyDecreaseP1: This is the key difference between KCHNG and regular money. KCHNG is designed to circulate, not accumulate.
about.whyDecreaseP2: When tokens sit idle in someone's wallet for too long, they gradually decrease in value. This isn't a punishment — it's how the system stays healthy:
about.whyDecreaseEncourage: Encourages spending — Use your KCHNG for meals, services, or trade
about.whyDecreaseHoarding: Prevents hoarding — Wealth can't just sit and grow automatically
about.whyDecreaseMoving: Keeps value moving — Active currency means more community interaction
about.whyDecreaseExample: Practical example: If you don't use your balance for a month, it might decrease by about 1%. Stay active and it stays full.
about.historyIntro: The idea of "active currency" isn't new. It's been tested and proven in real communities:
about.historyWorglDesc: A small town issued "work certificates" that lost value if held. Unemployment dropped dramatically, local trade flourished. The Austrian central bank eventually banned it.
about.historyChiemgauerDesc: A regional currency with a similar "use it or lose it" design. Still running after 20+ years, with money circulating about 3× faster than regular currency.
about.historyKchngDesc: Bringing this model to communities everywhere, using blockchain technology for transparency and trust.
about.historyQuote: "The Wörgl experiment demonstrated that active currency achieved a 12× velocity multiplier compared to conventional money — money changed hands 12 times more often, stimulating local trade and reducing unemployment."
about.historyCite: — Historical data from the Wörgl Freigeld experiment (1932-1933)
about.techIntro: KCHNG runs on Stellar, a public blockchain. This means:
about.techTransparent: Transparent — All transactions are public and verifiable
about.techNoCentral: No central authority — No single entity controls the currency
about.techOutro: You don't need to understand blockchain to use KCHNG. Just connect a wallet and start participating in your community's economy.
about.contractNote: Currently deployed contract addresses on Stellar:
about.walletCtaDesc: Connect your Stellar wallet to start earning and spending KCHNG in your community.
about.walletCtaNote: You'll need a Stellar-compatible wallet like Freighter to participate.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about KCHNG
faq.q1Q: What can I use KCHNG for?
faq.q1Intro: KCHNG can be used within your community for:
faq.q1a3: Goods at community markets or exchanges
faq.q1a4: Any transaction your community agrees to accept KCHNG for
faq.q1outro: The more your community adopts KCHNG, the more useful it becomes. It's a tool for internal exchange, not something you'd use at a regular store.
faq.q2Intro: You earn KCHNG by doing work that benefits your community:
faq.q2s1: Do the work — Cook a community meal, fix something, organize an event, clean shared spaces, teach a workshop, etc.
faq.q2s2: Submit a claim — Record your work in the system with evidence (photos, descriptions, etc.)
faq.q2s3: Get verification — Other community members confirm the work happened
faq.q2s4: Receive KCHNG — New tokens are minted directly to your wallet
faq.q2outro: The rate is simple: 30 minutes of standard work = 1000 KCHNG. Skilled work, teaching, or emergency work can earn multipliers (1.3×, 1.5×, or 2×).
faq.q3Q: Why do unused tokens decrease over time?
faq.q3p1: This is a feature, not a bug. KCHNG is designed to circulate, not sit idle.
faq.q3p2: When tokens aren't used for a period (typically after a month of inactivity), they gradually decrease in value — usually around 1% per month.
faq.q3a1: Encourages people to spend, share, or trade rather than hoard
faq.q3a2: Keeps the community economy active and healthy
faq.q3a4: Historical data shows this increases the "velocity" of money — it changes hands more often, creating more economic activity
faq.q3history: This model was proven in Wörgl (1932) and continues today with the Chiemgauer in Germany.
faq.q3good: Good news: If you're going through hardship, communities can grant "grace periods" that pause this effect temporarily.
faq.q4Q: Do I need technical knowledge to use KCHNG?
faq.q4No: No. You need to be able to:
faq.q4a2: Connect a digital wallet (like Freighter for Stellar)
faq.q4a3: Remember your wallet password or key
faq.q4outro: That's it. You don't need to understand blockchain, smart contracts, or any technical details. The system handles all of that in the background.
faq.q4banking: If you can use online banking, you can use KCHNG.
faq.q5p1: Work is verified by other community members, not algorithms or a central authority.
faq.q5a1: Someone does work and submits a claim with evidence (photos, descriptions, links)
faq.q5a2: Designated "verifiers" in the community review and approve the claim
faq.q5a3: Usually 2 verifiers are required to prevent abuse
faq.q5a4: Once approved, tokens are automatically minted
faq.q5outro: This works best in communities where people know each other. If you claim to have fixed the roof, someone probably saw you do it. Trust is built through relationships.
faq.q6p1: A "community" in KCHNG is a group of people who agree to use KCHNG together. Each community can have its own:
faq.q6a1: Rules — What work counts, how verification works
faq.q6a2: Demurrage rate — How fast unused tokens decrease (5-15% annually)
faq.q6outro: You can belong to multiple communities. For example, you might be in your housing cooperative's community and your town's community.
faq.q6note: <small>Note: You might see these called "trusts" in some places — the terms refer to the same concept.</small>
faq.q7p1: Technically, KCHNG runs on blockchain technology (Stellar) and uses crypto wallets. So yes, it's a digital token.
faq.q7a1: Not for speculation — KCHNG's value is tied to work, not markets
faq.q7a2: Not for investment — Tokens decrease when held, so there's no "HODL" mentality
faq.q7a3: Community-focused — Designed for local exchange, not global trading
faq.q7a4: Created by work — Not mined or pre-mined; new tokens only come from verified work
faq.q7outro: Think of it as "community credit on a blockchain" rather than "cryptocurrency."
faq.q8Q: How do I join a community?
faq.q8s1a1: Install Freighter (Stellar wallet browser extension) or another Stellar-compatible wallet
faq.q8s1a2: Create a new wallet and save your key somewhere safe
faq.q8s2a1: Browse existing communities on the communities page
faq.q8s2a2: Or ask your community if they already have a KCHNG setup
faq.q8s3a2: Existing members or admins will approve you
faq.q8s3a3: Once approved, you can submit work and earn KCHNG
faq.q8outro: No community yet? You can create your own — you just need a few other people who want to participate.
faq.q9Q: What if I lose my wallet?
faq.q9p1: This is important: Your KCHNG is stored on the blockchain, not in your wallet app. The wallet is just a key to access it.
faq.q9a1: If you have your key — You can restore access on any Stellar wallet
faq.q9a2: If you lose your key — Your KCHNG is permanently inaccessible
faq.q9p3: There's no "forgot password" feature. This is the tradeoff of decentralization — you have full control, which means you're fully responsible for keeping your key safe.
faq.q9best: Best practice: Write your key on paper and store it somewhere secure. Never share it with anyone.
faq.q10Q: Can I convert KCHNG to regular money?
faq.q10p1: Not directly. KCHNG is designed for community exchange, not conversion to fiat currency.
faq.q10p2: However, communities could establish their own exchange mechanisms if they want to. This would be a community decision, not a built-in feature.
faq.q10outro: The goal of KCHNG is to create a parallel economy within communities, not to replace national currency entirely.
faq.orAbout: Or learn more about how KCHNG works

tooltip.governorPanel: As a community Governor, you manage your community's parameters, designate successors, and oversee community health.
tooltip.governorStake: Your collateral is at risk if your reputation drops below the probation threshold.
tooltip.verifierPanel: As a Verifier, you review and approve/reject work claims from community members.
tooltip.verifierPendingClaims: These claims need your review. Timely verification helps keep the community economy flowing.
tooltip.verifierStake: Your stake (100,000 KCHNG) is held as collateral. It is returned in full on voluntary step-down.
tooltip.oraclePanel: As an Oracle, you can activate grace periods for community members experiencing hardship.
tooltip.oracleStake: Your oracle stake (5,000,000 KCHNG) is returnable on voluntary step-down.
tooltip.memberPanel: Your community membership status and contribution history.
tooltip.communityFund: The verifier fund is seeded by the governor (300K KCHNG) and sustained by 70% of local demurrage.
tooltip.genesisPool: The genesis pool receives 30% of all demurrage across all communities, funding genesis verifiers.
tooltip.electionStatus: Verifier elections require 40% quorum and 60% approval to pass.

roles.noneFound: No special roles detected for this account.

governance.title: Governance | KCHNG
governance.heading: Governance
governance.subtitle: Community-driven proposal and voting system

process.heading: Governance Process:
process.description: Proposals go through a 7-day review period, followed by a 3-day voting period. Approved proposals have a 30-day implementation notice. Rate changes require community governor approval; protocol changes require admin approval.

empty.title: No Proposals Yet
empty.description: Be the first to create a governance proposal!

voteModal.title: Cast Your Vote
voteModal.supportDesc: Vote in favor of this proposal

governance.valueFooter: Protocol: 30 min verified work → 1,000 KCHNG minted. Social peg: 1,000 KCHNG ≈ 1 meal.

tooltip.wallet: A digital wallet stores your KCHNG tokens and lets you send and receive payments on the Stellar blockchain.
tooltip.stellar: Stellar is a public blockchain network that KCHNG runs on. It provides fast, low-cost transactions.
tooltip.freighter: Freighter is a free browser extension wallet for Stellar. You can install it from freighter.app.
tooltip.testnet: Testnet is a testing network where tokens have no real value. Perfect for trying KCHNG without risk.
tooltip.demurrage: A gradual reduction of unused token balances over time, designed to encourage circulation and prevent hoarding. Active accounts are not affected.
tooltip.community: A community is a group of people who use KCHNG together, with their own verifiers and demurrage settings.
tooltip.verifier: A community member who reviews and approves or rejects work claims. Verifiers stake tokens to ensure honest verification. At least 2 verifiers are required per claim.
tooltip.oracle: A trusted community member who can activate grace periods for members experiencing hardship, such as emergencies or illness. Oracles stake tokens as a commitment to honest service.
tooltip.governance: The system by which community members propose, vote on, and implement changes to community parameters, rates, and roles.
tooltip.slashing: A penalty that deducts a portion of a role-holder's staked tokens for misconduct or poor performance. Verifiers face 10% slashing; oracles face 25%.
tooltip.minting: The creation of new KCHNG tokens as a direct reward for verified community work. Minting increases total supply without debiting any existing account.
tooltip.gracePeriod: A temporary pause on demurrage for a member experiencing hardship. Activated by an oracle for emergencies, illness, or by community vote. Limited to 3 per year.
tooltip.stake: Tokens locked as a commitment when taking on a trusted role (verifier, oracle). The stake is fully returnable when stepping down, but can be partially slashed for misconduct.
tooltip.balance: Your current KCHNG token balance. 1,000 KCHNG is minted per 30 minutes of verified community work.
tooltip.contributions: Your total verified community work hours and grace period usage for the current year.

terms.title: Glossary
terms.demurrageDef: A gradual reduction applied to inactive token balances. Encourages spending and circulation rather than hoarding.
terms.oracleDef: A trusted community role responsible for activating grace periods during member hardship. Requires a stake of 5,000,000 KCHNG.
terms.verifierDef: A community member who reviews work claims and votes to approve or reject them. Requires a stake of 100,000 KCHNG.
terms.governorDef: The community leader who manages membership, sets demurrage rates (within 5-15%), and administers verifiers.
terms.governanceDef: The proposal and voting system that lets community members collectively decide on changes.
terms.slashingDef: A penalty that removes a portion of a role-holder's stake for misconduct or poor performance.
terms.mintingDef: Creating new KCHNG tokens as a reward for verified work. 30 minutes of standard work mints 1,000 KCHNG.
terms.gracePeriodDef: A temporary pause on demurrage for members in hardship. Activated by an oracle or community vote. Limited to 3 per year.
terms.stakeDef: Tokens locked by a trusted role-holder as a commitment. Fully returnable on step-down, but subject to slashing for misconduct.
terms.workClaimDef: A submission recording community work performed, including type, duration, and evidence. Requires verifier approval to mint tokens.
terms.communityDef: A group of people using KCHNG together with shared rules, demurrage rates, and verifiers. Each community is independent.
terms.reputationDef: A per-role trust score (0-1000, starting at 500). Low scores can trigger probation with limited abilities and higher slashing rates.
terms.probationDef: A restricted state triggered when a role's reputation drops below 200. Limits abilities and increases slashing penalties.
terms.basisPointDef: One hundredth of a percent (0.01%). Demurrage rates are expressed in basis points for precision. 1,200 bps = 12%.
terms.crossCommunityExchangeDef: Transferring KCHNG between communities while preserving purchasing power. Exchange rates adjust for different demurrage rates.
terms.tf2tDef: A penalty detection mechanism: 2 or more consecutive negative events trigger an additional -25 bonus penalty. Prevents gaming through alternating behavior.
